description_long: This is an electric guitar with a distinctive V-shaped body, reminiscent of the Gibson Flying V design, featuring a bold red outline and a white faceplate. The body is angular and sharp, with two pointed wings and a flat central section where the bridge and pickups are located. The neck is long and black, leading to a rectangular headstock with four visible tuning pegs arranged in a straight line. The guitar's faceplate includes minimalistic black details, such as the bridge, pickups, and control knobs, all set against the white surface. The back and sides of the body are finished in a deep red color, contrasting with the white top. This instrument is likely constructed from solid wood with a glossy painted finish, and is designed for use in musical performances or as a decorative urban object.
description: A V-shaped electric guitar with a red and white body, black neck, and rectangular headstock. The design is bold and angular, with minimalistic hardware details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the white faceplate, red body outline, black neck, and rectangular headstock with tuning pegs.
description_view_1: Angled top-down view highlighting the V-shaped body, white faceplate, and the arrangement of hardware components.
description_view_2: Rear-side view emphasizing the red back and the thickness of the body, with the neck extending outward.
description_view_3: Side profile showing the slimness of the guitar body and the straight alignment of the neck.
attribute_headstock_design: Rectangular headstock with four tuning pegs in a straight line, no visible logo
